title: Samsung opens ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex access after AI restrictions
slides: Why did Samsung reverse its AI ban?
It now allows ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex.
Restrictions once blocked all generative AI use.
ChatGPT Enterprise now secures sensitive company data.
Test internal prompts against your data privacy policy.
thread_text:
1. Why the reversal?

Samsung employees accidentally leaked data via ChatGPT, forcing a total ban. Now they’re letting it back in.

2. The new rule

Only ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex are approved. Personal accounts still blocked.

3. The old wall

Last year Samsung banned all generative AI. Worried about code and secrets ending up in training data.

4. What changed

Enterprise promises data isolation. Your company’s prompts won’t train the model.

5. Your move

Grab a real internal prompt and test it against your data privacy policy today. Have you checked?
video_script: Samsung reversed its AI ban after data leaks.

script: Samsung reversed its AI ban after data leaks.
Now only ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex are allowed.
Old restrictions blocked all generative AI tools.
Enterprise secures your sensitive company data.
Test internal prompts against your privacy policy.

instagram.caption:
Samsung just reopened the door to ChatGPT—but only its Enterprise version, not the free chatbot your team probably used. 🚪

Employees once leaked secret code and data, triggering a total ban on all generative AI. Now the company trusts Enterprise to keep your prompts separate from model training. 🛡️ No more accidental leaks into public AI.

For anyone writing code, internal docs, or sensitive content: your work can stay private again. But only if you use the approved tool.

**Grab one actual work prompt right now—does it violate your own privacy policy?** 🧾

Data isolation means your company prompts won't train the model, securing sensitive information from exposure.
